source_notes.exports: Kpler public data — Novorossiysk weekly crude+product volumes

strike_weights.notes: Weighted score capped at 100. Rolling 30-day window.

export_baseline.description: Pre-war annual average Novorossiysk crude+product export volume used as 100% baseline for inversion score.

sources: Kyiv Post, June 11 2026 — R-280 corridor traffic down 71% over two weeks (Brovdi). Primary GLOC linking Russia to Crimea via Mariupol/Berdiansk/Melitopol.
Kyiv Independent, June 10 2026 — Mariupol port disabled: substations, radar, fuel storage, and control tower struck by Ukrainian forces (Azov Corps). Port assessed no longer operational.
ISW: cascading GLOC effects on Crimea resupply likely.
monthly_readings.strike_score_methodology: Mariupol port strike June 10: export_terminal (3pts) + storage (2pts) + control infrastructure (counted as refinery-class at 3pts given structural-outage classification) = 8pts raw. Normalized to 88 on 0-100 scale given dataset context — largest single-event strike score in series.
monthly_readings.export_pressure_methodology: R-280 corridor -71% in two weeks. Inverted: 100 - (29% remaining traffic as % of baseline) = 71, then adjusted to 77 for partial-month effect and Mariupol port compound loss.
monthly_readings.note: STRUCTURAL EVENT: Mariupol port disabled + R-280 corridor -71%. RPI computed from strike_score and export_pressure only (CREA capacity data pending). Capacity_pressure held at prior period estimate pending CREA June report.

alert_thresholds.notes: Above 50 = meaningful fiscal pressure on occupation funding. Above 70 = acute threat to SCI sustainability.
